pattern: Convert an integer to a positional numeral system by repeated division and remainder, handling the sign separately.
hints: Zero is a special case; otherwise work with the magnitude and remember whether the value was negative.
Repeatedly take the remainder when divided by seven to get the least-significant digit, then divide by seven and continue.
Collect the remainders, reverse them into a string, and prepend a minus sign if the original number was negative.

edge_inputs.description: zero converts to a single zero digit
smallest base-7 digit that needs no carry
exactly seven rolls into a new place
negative magnitude one keeps the sign

stress.description: uniformly random signed integers across the full magnitude range
stress.note: size ignored; covers sign and magnitude
